description: Marsh & McLennan, now branding as Marsh, is a global professional services company spanning insurance brokerage, reinsurance advisory, workforce and investment consulting, and management consulting.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: Marsh & McLennan sits at the intersection of risk, capital, workforces, and strategy: a tidy corporate way of saying it helps large organizations price uncertainty, transfer it, model it, insure it, reinsure it, consult around it, and occasionally rename the department doing the consulting.

The company operates through major businesses including Marsh, Mercer, Guy Carpenter, and Oliver Wyman. Its registry profile should therefore treat Marsh and Mercer as seed products, while also evaluating Guy Carpenter and Oliver Wyman as likely product or business-unit entries during structured sync.

overview.title: Market Role
paragraphs: Marsh is one of the largest global insurance brokerage and risk advisory platforms, serving corporations, public entities, and institutions that need placement, risk management, analytics, and advisory services across complex insurance markets.

Mercer extends the company’s reach into health, wealth, retirement, talent, and investment consulting. The result is a services portfolio that touches both institutional risk transfer and employee-benefit infrastructure, which makes the company more operationally embedded than a conventional financial-sector label might suggest.

overview.title: Registry Implications
paragraphs: For Free The World, the main mapping challenge is not consumer lock-in but institutional dependency: Marsh & McLennan’s products are advisory, brokerage, actuarial, and data-driven services that shape procurement and risk decisions for enterprises rather than end-user software choices.

Potential alternative mapping should look for open, cooperative, public-interest, or decentralized approaches to insurance transparency, benefits administration, risk modeling, procurement data, catastrophe analytics, and investment-advisory infrastructure. None should be forced into the profile merely because a dashboard exists somewhere with a login screen.
